title: Violation
description: One check result: a single detected violation of a rule, with enough measured/required/location detail to reproduce and click-to-zoom. Emitted by every verifier and lint check; the universal output currency of the toolkit.

rule_id.description: Identifier of the rule (or check) that was violated. For connectivity checks with no deck rule, the check name (e.g. 'bijection.multi_assignment').

severity.description: Severity inherited from the rule. 'hard' violations drive nonzero CLI exit codes.

location.description: Where the violation is, physically (layer + coordinates) or logically (node/net).

measured.description: The value actually observed (e.g. measured trace width). Omitted for non-metric violations such as a role swap.

required.description: The value the rule requires (the threshold). Omitted for non-metric violations.

delta.description: Signed shortfall/excess (measured - required) in the measurement units, when both are numeric.

citation.description: Anchor back to the rule's source document, so a reviewer can find the governing text.

fix_hint.description: Optional free-text remediation suggestion. Advisory only; never auto-applied.

check_version.description: Semantic version of the check implementation that produced this violation, for reproducibility.

layer.description: Layer name the violation is on.

x.description: X coordinate (in the emitting tool's output units, conventionally um).

extent.description: Optional bounding box of the violating geometry, anchored at (x, y).

oneOf.description: A non-numeric measured/required value (e.g. an expected vs actual role like 'gnd').

sheet.description: Spreadsheet sheet name, for rule-sheet-derived rules.
